questions.question: What problem with DCGAN's latent space did StyleGAN solve?
options: The latent vector was too short
z controlled everything — pose, lighting, identity, background — entangled together; moving along one axis changed all four, so you couldn't ask for "same person, different pose"
z had to be an integer vector
DCGAN couldn't output color
questions.explanation: The fix: stop feeding z into the conv stack. Map z through an 8-layer MLP to an intermediate w, start synthesis from a learned constant 4×4×512 tensor, and inject w at every resolution.

questions.question: How does AdaIN inject style into the synthesis network?
options: By concatenating w to every feature map
Normalize each conv feature map (subtract mean, divide std), then scale and shift it by affine projections of w — "style" is literally the first- and second-order statistics of the feature map
By replacing convolution kernels entirely
Through attention over the latent bank
questions.explanation: Per-layer single-channel noise (with learned scaling) adds the stochastic detail — skin pores, hair strands — without touching global structure.

questions.question: What does W-space disentanglement enable?
options: Faster training only

Roughly orthogonal axes for high-level style (pose, identity) vs fine style (lighting, color) — you can mix image A's w at low resolutions with image B's at high, unlocking editing, stylization, and the whole StyleGAN-inversion research line
Lossless image compression
Automatic dataset labeling
questions.explanation: W isn't forced to be Gaussian — the mapping network learns a data-adapted shape, which is exactly what lets the axes factor more cleanly than in Z.

questions.question: What is the truncation trick?
options: Cutting the network to fewer layers
At inference, pull each w toward the mean ŵ via w' = ŵ + ψ·(w − ŵ); ψ < 1 trades diversity for quality — nearly every StyleGAN demo uses ψ ≈ 0.7
Truncating gradients during training
Cropping output images to squares
questions.explanation: Samples near the latent mean are more typical and higher quality; samples far out are diverse but riskier. ψ is the dial between them.

questions.question: What did StyleGAN2 and StyleGAN3 each fix?
options: Both just added more parameters

StyleGAN2 replaced AdaIN with weight demodulation (fixing droplet artifacts) and added path-length regularization; StyleGAN3 made convolutions alias-free and equivariant, eliminating textures sticking to the pixel grid
They added text conditioning
They switched to diffusion sampling
questions.explanation: StyleGAN3 remains the 2026 default for narrow-domain photorealism at high FPS, few-shot domain adaptation, and inversion-based editing — but for open-domain text-to-image, diffusion is the tool.
